prompt: I'm writing an ESP32 sensor node that reads a BME280 over I2C and publishes to MQTT every 5 seconds. It also has a deep sleep mode to save power — it should wake up, read sensor, publish, and go back to sleep. But after deep sleep wake-up, the WiFi reconnection takes 3-5 seconds and sometimes fails. The whole wake cycle should be under 2 seconds. I'm using Arduino framework. How do I optimize this?

assertions.name: rtc_memory_for_wifi
assertions.description: Uses RTC_DATA_ATTR to store WiFi channel and BSSID across deep sleep cycles

assertions.name: static_ip_optimization
assertions.description: Recommends static IP to eliminate DHCP negotiation time

assertions.name: proper_deep_sleep_api
assertions.description: Uses esp_deep_sleep_start() or ESP.deepSleep() with correct timer configuration

assertions.name: sensor_forced_mode
assertions.description: Addresses BME280 forced/single-shot mode for power-efficient readings

assertions.name: provides_working_code
assertions.description: Includes concrete code for the optimized wake-read-publish-sleep cycle
